title: amoria physodes
side_effects: Amoria physodes can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing excessive bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties, or triggering allergic reactions, which can lead to symptoms like hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use of the plant can also lead to kidney damage and decreased platelet count, increasing the risk of bleeding disorders. In rare cases, Amoria physodes can cause anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction, if ingested or applied topically without proper medical supervision. If not handled correctly, the plant's toxins can also be absorbed through the skin, causing skin irritation, rashes, and lesions.
